zh.Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾馬波索普
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Aske於大約1364年出生在英國越可爾豪登時，她的父親John de Aske33歲，母親Joan Shelvestrode23歲。她和Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III至少有2個兒子。她去世於1403年6月9日在英國林可爾史爾享年40歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾馬波索普。

zh-hans.Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England: 英国林可爾史爾馬波索普
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Aske于大约1364年出生在英国越可爾豪登时，她的父亲John de Aske33岁，母亲Joan Shelvestrode23岁。她和Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III至少有2个儿子。她去世于1403年6月9日在英国林可爾史爾享年40岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾馬波索普。

nameMeanings.name: Aske
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Staffordshire): topographic name from northern Middle English ask ‘ash tree’ (Old Norse askr), or a habitational name from Aske (North Yorkshire). Compare Ash .

variants.name: Ash
meanings: English: from Middle English asche ‘ash tree’ (Old English æsc), hence a topographic name for someone living by an ash tree or a habitational name from any of the many places in southern and central England named with this word (Derbyshire, Dorset, Hampshire, Herefordshire, Kent, Surrey, Shropshire, Somerset, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of German   Asch   and   Esch  , the latter ultimately also of Swiss German origin (see   Oesch  ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dufresne  , with the same meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Axe
meanings: English (West Yorkshire, Lincolnshire): variant of Aske with metathesis of /sk/ to /ks/, spelled with an x.

English (Dorset, Somerset): habitational name from Axe Farm in Chardstock (Devon, formerly in Dorset), itself named for the river Axe, which forms part of the south-eastern boundary of Chardstock parish.
Americanized form of German   Axt   or perhaps of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Aske was born about 1364, in Howden, Yorkshire, England, her father, John de Aske, was 33 and her mother, Joan Shelvestrode, was 23. She had at least 2 sons with Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III. She died on 9 June 1403, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Aske est née environ 1364, à Howden, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John de Aske, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan Shelvestrode, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III. Elle est décédée le 9 juin 1403, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterrée à Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Aske um 1364 in Howden, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Aske 33 und ihre Mutter Joan Shelvestrode 23. Sie hatte mit Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 9. Juni 1403 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Aske è nata il circa 1364 nel Howden, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, John de Aske, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Joan Shelvestrode, aveva 23 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III. Morì il 9 giugno 1403, nel Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolta a Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England: イギリスリンコルンシュアイアマブレソルペ
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Askeが約1364年にイギリスヨークシュアイアホーウデンで生まれた時，父John de Askeは33歳，母Joan Shelvestrodeは23歳であった。彼女はSir Thomas FitzWilliam IIIとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1403年6月9日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで40歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアマブレソルペにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Aske(이)가 대략 1364년 영국 요르크실르 하우든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Aske(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Shelvestrode(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1403년 6월 9일 영국 링코실르에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 마블레소에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Aske nasceu aproximadamente 1364, em Howden, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Aske, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Joan Shelvestrode, tinha 23 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III. Ela faleceu em 9 de junho de 1403, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 40 anos, e foi sepultada em Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Aske родилась около 1364 г. в местности хоуден, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Aske было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Shelvestrode, 23. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III. Она умерла 9 июня 1403 г. в местности линколншир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и была похоронена в местности маблетаурп, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Aske nació aproximadamente 1364, en Howden, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Aske, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Joan Shelvestrode, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Sir Thomas FitzWilliam III. Murió el 9 de junio de 1403, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultada en Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
